item.title: "If you can't beat them, join them"
item.dc:creator: Hauntologist2

item.title: Shout out to all those of us in healthy unconventional marriages
item.description: Sorry, I'm just wanted to talk for a minute So my husband and I have been together for almost 15 years. Been married for 11 years. The night before we got married, I told my bridal party, "If hubby ever came out as gay, I would stand by him." I am usually pretty good at reading people, and yeah, I thought my hubby was in the closet. But you know what, he was an amazing partner. We had a long engagement and started living together soon after dating. Through the years, we have been each others support for a lot of major events. My hubby losing his grandfather and uncle (his family is very close), me losing my dad, us losing a child together, and me separating myself from very toxic and narcissistic family members. I can, with 100% honestly, say that no one knows me better or has been more supportive of my choices and goals. We have 2 beautiful children, who are both autistic. My hubby is an amazing father and truly helps with both parenting and things around the house. A couple of years ago, hubby came out as bi. After a lot of conversations and months, we decided to open our relationship. Hubby tried coming out as a teenager, and his parents' response was to take him to talk with the family pastor. They aren't super religious, they just didn't know what to do. We live in a small town in the Midwest. You can guess what he heard from those around him about being gay. So he stayed in the closet. I told him that after a while, if he decided he would rather be with men, I would again support him. I told him I loved him, and he deserved to be himself and live a life that made him happy. We have spent the last several years working on our communication and creating an environment where we both feel safe to come to each other with any issue. This is a very important foundation in our relationship to both of us. It is something we both work hard to always keep improving both for each other and for our children. Neither of us grew up in homes where our parents were good models of communication. My parents yelled and screamed and his would just not talk to each. Hubby found a partner a couple of months after coming out as bi. A few months ago, hubby came out as gay. Again after a lot of discussion we decided we didn't want to get divorced. We still loved each other, we didn't want to put each other through a divorce, and didn't want to put the kids through it. We have a large house so we can each have our own bedroom and space. I love my hubby's partner. And I also now have a boyfriend. Both of our partners know the situation. In fact my hubby considers my boyfriend a good friend of his. I often talk with hubby's boyfriend a lot during the day. We will video chat for hours. Everyone is aware of the situation and everyone is comfortable with it. Is it conventional? Hell no. Does it work? Yes and very well. I am raising my children together with an amazing partner. I come home to someone who supports me, emotionally, physically, and spirituality. (I know people who are married and cant say this) My partner gets the same. My kids have two uncles who understand their special needs and are amazing with them. Thank you for letting me rant! Sorry if this skips around. My adhd is strong today Edit: Thank you everyone for your lovely comments. I am at a loss of word from the support here. I am going to say to anyone who was in my hubby's spot a few years ago and not sure if they should come out, I will tell you what I still tell him today, "You are amazing and you deserve to live a life that you are happy with and that makes you comfortable". BIG LOVE TO YOU ALL. ❤️
item.dc:creator: SailorMongoose85

item.title: Megara Cosplay
item.description:
SnarkyJayCosplay here— finally did a photo shoot as my favorite Disney leading lady: Megara. This is the first wig I styled (actually splicing together a wig, ponytail clip, etc) so I’m really proud!

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1058 (Surprise)
item.description:
Hey, it's that time of day again! Hooray! I get to be a problem for the world. Let's get this bread.
I would like capitalism to collapse now, thanks.
Female ducks aren't nearly as easy to tell apart as their male bretheren.
Use this card wisely, today.
*insert Jay screaming noises*
The cranium of a new pachycephalosaur from the Lance formation recently was published that shows that, even after the lumping of Dracorex/Stygomoloch into Pachycephalosaurus [which isn't accepted by *all* paleontologists] that there was a second species in the ecosystem after all, it's that different.
"WHEN THE WINGED--WHAT THE FUCK?!"
Triassic scene from the Chinlie Formation of the Colorado Plateau, by Brian Engh
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "I love you. Step up. Okay."

item.title: Pastafarians unite under his noodley goodness!
item.description: I welcome all the join the Church of the Flying Spaghetti Monster in its annual celebrations. The church is open to all. There is no cost to join. We have members in 60 countries around the world and recognition by multiple states in the USA and in a number of countries as a valid religion. Right now they have a free holiday card feature at the site to send an appropriate card to your friends and family. ( Google "Church of the Flying Spaghetti Monster" to find the site if you're interested. I don't want to SPAM here). -- May you be touched by his noodley goodness this holiday season! Spread the gospel of FSM wherever you go! The Rev. RetrogradeLlama
item.dc:creator: RetrogradeLlama

item.title: Per My Last Dump…
item.description:
Hey all! It’s me, your friendly Imgurhood trans gal :) Per one of my recent posts I talked about what peoples’ trans Realizations (etc) were like. Then tons of people started sharing their own experiences! *(Nobody that I know of must read my commentary. If you’re one, more power to you, friend! Just skip these and enjoy the memes)
Then the brilliant idea to compile all of these user stories into an anthology appeared! So, here’s your ‘Iron Will: a trans love letter’ progress report…
;) Upon reviewing, there’s a shit ton of stories - which is awesome. But. There’s also a shit ton of stories - which need acquiring, arranging, and formatting! (It’s ok, this is a good problem to have).
I gotta say, of everyone who submitted, even those who ultimately did not want their stories included (Respect), I am so proud of each and every one of you.
I’m in the middle of tedious reviewal of permissions, copy pasting, and arranging - along with minor minor grammatical and spelling edits.
Already, we’re waaaay over the minimum 42 pages required for indie publishing :)
Once I’m there, I’ll post a teaser rough draft, then have pre-ordering up and running!
Yeah, I did say, ‘for sale’. I intend to continually donate a portion of the proceedings to a wonderful pro lgbtq+ organization called Free Mom Hugs. You can learn more about them here: https://freemomhugs.org
Honestly, I gotta add that all your stories are helping me too. I’m fresh to all this, so reading and learning from others who have trailblazers ahead is a truly, deeply, moving thing for me.
Thank You :)
Per my previous promise, I’ll keep you up to date as progress continues.
If you’re just now learning about this endeavor and would like your own lgbtq+ Realization story included, just DM me with it :)
Already, each Realization story is complex, humanizing, and sometimes haunting.
This is the world we live in today.
It can be dark, scary, and repressive.
So I say turn on all the lights.
Knowledge and community are Power.
You’re not alone in this.
You never were.
And you’re fucking awesome.
And others need to know it so they too can realize that they’re pretty cool themselves.
Ok, there. That’s it. That’s the update ;)
Now, please enjoy the rest of these weird ass memes!
MOST VIRAL: Awwww, look at you all… upvote’n my stuff and what not… you rock :) Be excellent to each other! Love, Your friendly Imgurhood trans gal :) *obligatory author site link: https://ryanpfreeman.com
item.dc:creator: RyanMcFlyin
